skill.name: subagent-driven-development
skill.description: Coordinate implementation tasks by dispatching focused subagents, then reviewing each task for specification compliance and code quality.
skill.summary: Run independent implementation tasks through focused subagents and review loops.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but the reviewed files are markdown workflow templates. The backtick, weak crypto, reconnaissance, and hidden path alerts are false positives; the remaining concern is normal agentic code editing and commit activity.

low_findings.title: Agentic Repository Modification Workflow
low_findings.description: The skill asks implementer subagents to implement tasks, write tests, verify work, and commit changes. This is expected for a coding workflow, but it can modify local files and git history.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The prompt explicitly tells subagents to implement, test, commit, and report changed files. This confirms local modification capability, but no malicious intent or exfiltration is present.

low_findings.title: Static Command And Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance matches occur inside markdown examples, graph diagrams, prompt templates, or SHA review metadata. I found no executable script, shell command, cryptographic operation, or network action.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant files contain fenced markdown and natural language templates, not runnable code. The static labels align with syntax tokens and review terms rather than security-sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: Illustrative Hidden Home Directory Path
low_findings.description: The home-directory path is part of an example user answer about where to install a hook. It does not instruct the agent to read, write, or exfiltrate hidden files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 107 is clearly dialogue in an example workflow. It is not executable code and does not request sensitive file access.

content.value_statement: Complex implementation plans often lose quality when one agent carries too much context. This skill splits independent tasks across fresh subagents and adds review gates after each task.
actual_capabilities: Dispatches a fresh implementer subagent for each independent task.
Provides prompt templates for implementation, specification review, and code quality review.
Requires the controller to extract full task context before delegation.
Defines review loops until specification and quality issues are resolved.
Guides final review after all planned tasks are complete.
limitations: It depends on the host agent supporting subagents or task delegation.
It does not execute code by itself or provide automation scripts.
It works best when tasks are independent and clearly specified.
It can increase token use because each task receives multiple reviews.

use_cases.title: Execute a multi-task feature plan
use_cases.description: Break a prepared feature plan into independent task executions with review after each task.

use_cases.title: Keep implementation aligned with requirements
use_cases.description: Use a dedicated reviewer to compare completed work against the original task text before judging code quality.

use_cases.title: Coordinate focused coding agents
use_cases.description: Give each subagent limited context and reduce confusion across long development sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Run one prepared task
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for one clear task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use subagent-driven-development for this task. I will provide the full task text, context, working directory, and acceptance criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Execute a small implementation plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Several independent tasks from a plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Read this plan once, extract each independent task, create a task list, and execute them through implementer, spec reviewer, and code quality reviewer subagents.

prompt_templates.title: Review a completed subagent task
prompt_templates.scenario: Check whether a subagent completed a task correctly.
prompt_templates.prompt: Dispatch the spec compliance reviewer for this task. Compare the actual implementation to the requested task and report missing or extra work with file references.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate full branch completion
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow after all tasks are implemented.
prompt_templates.prompt: After all subagent tasks pass spec and quality review, dispatch a final code reviewer for the whole branch and summarize remaining release risks.

output_examples.input: Run the first task from my feature plan using subagents.
output: Creates a task entry with the full task text and context.
Dispatches an implementer subagent and captures questions before work begins.
Starts spec review and code quality review after implementation is reported.

output_examples.input: The spec reviewer found missing progress reporting.
output: Returns the task to the implementer subagent for a focused fix.
Requests another spec review after the fix is complete.
Delays code quality review until specification compliance passes.

output_examples.input: All tasks from the plan are complete.
output: Dispatches a final reviewer for the whole implementation.
Checks that all task-level reviews passed.
Prepares the branch for the finishing workflow.

best_practices: Provide each subagent the full task text and only the context it needs.
Run specification review before code quality review for every task.
Keep review loops open until each reported issue is fixed and rechecked.
anti_patterns: Do not dispatch multiple implementer subagents against the same files in parallel.
Do not ask subagents to infer requirements from a large plan file.
Do not accept a self-review as a replacement for independent review.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It coordinates implementation tasks through fresh subagents and adds two review stages after each task.

faq.question: Does it run scripts automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides workflow instructions and prompt templates for an agent that supports subagent delegation.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when you already have a plan with independent implementation tasks.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is written for tools that can delegate focused tasks to subagents.

faq.question: Why are there two reviewers?
faq.answer: The first reviewer checks specification compliance. The second reviewer checks code quality after compliance passes.

faq.question: What is the main risk?
faq.answer: The workflow can direct agents to edit files and commit changes, so users should review results before merging.
